af_score_components.notes: XcodeBuildMCP enables AI agents to build, test, run, and debug iOS/macOS Xcode projects. Has MCP quality 85 (Sentry-backed). No auth required (local). macOS 14.5+ and Xcode 16.x required. Code signing must be pre-configured. Sentry telemetry opt-out required. Per-workspace daemon state can get stale. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — local MCP server. Code signing handled separately by Xcode (good separation). Sentry telemetry is opt-out only — sends build telemetry to Sentry by default (privacy concern). Local execution with current user's filesystem permissions. Sentry-maintained project brings enterprise security awareness. No compliance certifications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: 46 releases indicating active, stable development. Sentry-backed project provides reliability confidence. Per-workspace daemon can get stale. macOS/Xcode version constraints. Partial idempotency. No SLA as open-source tool. Well-documented for Apple developer workflows. Build automation reliability depends on Xcode stability.

af_score_components.notes: Xero cloud accounting API. No MCP server. OAuth2 with PKCE required — 30-minute access token expiry (very short, frequent refresh needed). 60 calls/minute limit is tight for batch operations. Tenant ID required in all requests. Multi-tenant agents must track tenant context. OpenAPI spec available. auth_complexity=40 for OAuth2 PKCE with 30min expiry requiring very frequent refresh.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with PKCE prevents code interception attacks. Granular scopes per API (accounting, payroll, assets, projects). 30-minute access token expiry reduces breach window. SOC2/GDPR/ISO27001 certified. Refresh tokens valid 60 days. Partner apps require Xero certification. Strong security for financial data.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2/ISO27001 certified with status page. Retry guidance documented. 30-minute token expiry requires reliable refresh logic. 60 req/min rate limit needs careful management. Offset pagination. Partial idempotency. Archived records affect response counts. Financial year varies by country. Strong platform reliability for accounting use cases.

af_score_components.notes: XHS-Downloader for Xiaohongshu content download with MCP. Has MCP quality 65. Cookie-based auth (optional for enhanced video quality). Python 3.12+ required. Platform API changes can break without notice. Platform ToS may prohibit automated scraping. Built-in throttling must be respected. auth_complexity=85 for optional cookie-based auth.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cookie-based auth (browser session cookies). HTTPS for Xiaohongshu API calls. No formal security review. Platform ToS compliance is user responsibility. Cookie storage in local config file. Proxy support (HTTP/SOCKS). No compliance certifications. Scraping tool with inherent platform-dependent security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on Xiaohongshu platform API stability — can break without notice. Full idempotency for download operations (good). Built-in throttling helps avoid IP blocks. No SLA. Python 3.12+ requirement. Active development. Platform ToS risk to long-term availability. Community-maintained.

af_score_components.notes: Xiaohongshu MCP for content publishing and management. Has MCP quality 60. Manual browser login required on first use — cannot be fully automated. Single session per account (concurrent agent use breaks session). Max 50 posts/day. 20-char title limit, 1000-char body limit. Go/Chromium headless. auth_complexity=55 for manual browser login requirement reducing agent automation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Browser session cookies for auth. Manual login requirement. Single concurrent session limits attack surface. Headless Chromium execution. No formal security review. Platform ToS risk (automated posting may violate Xiaohongshu ToS). Account ban risk with heavy automation. No compliance certifications. Community project.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Headless browser automation is inherently fragile to platform changes. Single session constraint limits scaling. 50 posts/day self-enforced limit. Platform API changes can break without notice. No SLA. Account suspension risk reduces reliability for production use. Manual login breaks full automation. Community-maintained Go project.

af_score_components.notes: Xiaozhi ESP32 Server for AI voice assistant hardware. Has MCP quality 55. Username/password web dashboard. Project EXPLICITLY STATES it has not passed security evaluation. Requires 2-8GB RAM. Multiple LLM/TTS/ASR API keys needed. Chinese-language documentation primarily. ESP32 hardware dependency. auth_complexity=80 for username/password but project warns against production security use.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: Project explicitly states it has NOT passed security evaluation. Username/password auth only. No compliance certifications. IoT device integration adds attack surface. Multiple external API keys needed for LLM/TTS/ASR providers. Self-hosted with user-managed security. Not suitable for any production or internet-exposed deployment without significant security hardening. Existing security 30.0 reflects this.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted IoT backend — reliability entirely user-managed. 2-8GB RAM requirement significant for edge deployments. Hardware (ESP32) dependency. Multiple external API dependencies. Chinese-language docs limit international support. Active development with community use. No SLA. Educational/maker project not for production.

af_score_components.notes: Xiaozhi ESP32 Java/Spring Boot server for enterprise IoT device management. Has MCP quality 60. Username/password auth. MySQL + Redis required (higher operational complexity). MQTT support only in commercial version (open source limited to WebSocket). Performance benchmarks at 100 concurrent devices. Chinese-language primary docs. auth_complexity=75 for username/password with Java security config.

security_score_components.security_notes: Spring Boot security framework provides baseline protection. MySQL + Redis require separate security configuration. Username/password auth without MFA. IoT device authentication management via web dashboard. Commercial MQTT features limited in open source. No compliance certifications. Self-hosted deployment requires operator security hardening. Better than Python sibling but still not production-security-audited.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Spring Boot provides mature runtime stability. MySQL + Redis dependency adds operational requirements. Commercial MQTT limitation affects real-time device communication. 100 concurrent device benchmarks only. Self-hosted reliability is operator-managed. No SLA. Chinese-language docs limit international support. Active development with enterprise Java ecosystem.

af_score_components.notes: Yokai Go backend framework with MCP server support. Has MCP quality 70. No prescribed auth (framework). MCP support is relatively new with fewer examples. Uber Fx dependency injection has learning curve. Built-in OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics. HTTP, gRPC, MCP, and worker patterns supported. auth_complexity=100 as framework — auth is application responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: Framework provides security-ready primitives but auth is developer responsibility. Go memory safety benefits. OpenTelemetry for security observability. HTTPS support via Echo middleware. Viper for secrets configuration management. No built-in auth — applications must implement using Echo middleware. MIT licensed with commercial friendliness. Active maintenance from Ankorstore.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Mature Go framework with 822 stars. Ankorstore-backed (e-commerce company using in production). Built-in health checks, structured logging, OpenTelemetry metrics. HTTP, gRPC, MCP, and worker patterns all supported. No SLA as open-source. MCP support newer than HTTP/gRPC support. Conventional commits for reliable release automation.

af_score_components.notes: YouTube Data API v3. No MCP server. API key for public read-only, OAuth2 for user operations. Quota system is complex: search list costs 100 units vs 1 for video fetch. Daily quota resets at midnight Pacific. OAuth tokens expire and must be refreshed. Caption retrieval requires additional scopes. Content ID API is separate restricted program. auth_complexity=70 for dual auth with quota complexity.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced (Google infrastructure). API key for public data, OAuth2 for user context with granular scopes. GDPR/COPPA compliant. Google's enterprise security infrastructure. OAuth2 PKCE for client apps. API key via query parameter is a concern for public-data access (visible in URLs). User context operations well-protected by OAuth2.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google infrastructure — industry-leading uptime. Cursor pagination. Retry guidance documented. Quota system is the main reliability challenge (daily reset, complex unit costs). Daily quota resets at midnight Pacific (not rolling). API versioned (v3 stable for years). Multi-language SDK officially maintained. Content ID restrictions may limit some use cases.

af_score_components.notes: Zapier workflow automation API. No MCP server. OAuth2 for platform integrations, webhook URL secrets for triggers. Free tier Zaps fire every 15 minutes (not real-time). Async processing — no immediate results from triggered Zaps. Webhook URLs are single-use secrets. Task counting per-action (multi-step Zaps consume multiple tasks). auth_complexity=65 for OAuth2 + webhook URL security.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 for platform integrations with scoped access. Webhook URLs are secrets (treat as credentials). SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA compliant. GDPR compliance requires data residency settings (not default). No webhook signing on incoming webhooks by default. Partner API uses OAuth2. Enterprise plan includes additional security controls.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2/HIPAA certified with status page. Free tier 15-minute polling limits real-time use. Async processing means no immediate feedback. Cursor pagination. No idempotency support. Limited Zap execution history retention. Task counting per-action affects cost modeling. Enterprise plans offer faster polling and higher execution limits.

af_score_components.notes: Zen self-hosted notes app with optional MCP. Has MCP quality 60 (experimental). No documented authentication — relies on network-level access control. MCP server is experimental. Semantic search requires separate 'Zen Intelligence' companion service. Single-binary Go app with SQLite. AGPL-3.0 license. auth_complexity=90 as no auth required (but this is a security risk).

security_score_components.security_notes: No documented authentication — significant security concern for any network-exposed deployment. Relies on network-level access control (suitable only for trusted local networks). AGPL license requires source disclosure for modified network services. SQLite database has no encryption. Go single-binary reduces complexity vulnerabilities. Not suitable for internet exposure without additional auth layer. Existing security 45.0 reflects auth absence.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted single binary — reliability is user-managed. Experimental MCP server with no stability guarantees. Semantic search requires additional companion service. No SLA. SQLite provides durable local storage. Docker support for containerized deployment. AGPL license requires careful compliance for network deployments. Suitable for personal/trusted-network use only.
